title: The Monk Who Told the Modern World to Thirst for God
usage_rules: Prefer Wikimedia Commons categories and file pages with explicit license/public-domain information.
For modern icons/photos, keep attribution and license in asset_attribution.json.
Do not assume every icon image is free just because the saint is old; verify the file page license.
Use icons reverently: no distorted faces, no mocking zooms, no sensational effects.
Mix icon/portrait assets with monastery exteriors, manuscripts, maps, candles, and landscape footage to avoid a static slideshow.

scenes.title: The Path to the Hut
scenes.visual_prompt: Reverent cinematic Mount Athos footpath at dawn, humble elder's hut in the distance, sea mist, prayerful atmosphere, no sensationalism.

beats.narration_excerpt: The path was not impressive.  It was not built for crowds, cameras, or fame.  It was a rough path on Mount Athos, leading toward the cell of a monk who owned al...
beats.asset_note: Opening/scene anchor: use Mount Athos path, cell, monastery, sea, or reverent Athonite atmosphere.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: And yet people climbed it carrying questions they could barely say out loud.  Some came anxious.  Some came ashamed.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: path climbed carrying questions barely loud anxious Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: Some came with grief.  Some came because modern life had become loud enough to make the soul feel thirsty.  At the end of the path was Elder Paisios.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: path grief because modern become loud enough Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: He did not offer a slogan.  He did not offer a performance.  He listened, prayed, and kept pointing people toward one question: what does your soul truly thirst...
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: path offer slogan performance listened prayed kept Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: He sought silence.  He worked with his hands.  He learned that the Christian life was not an idea kept safely in a book.

scenes.title: The Mountain That Hides Men
scenes.visual_prompt: Mount Athos monastery cliffs, night vigil candles, monk walking alone with prayer rope, dark sea below, reverent documentary style.

beats.narration_excerpt: Mount Athos has a way of hiding people from the world and revealing them to God.  Paisios did not go there to become famous.

beats.narration_excerpt: He went to pray, obey, repent, and be formed.  The Holy Mountain is not romantic when seen from the inside.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: mountain hides went pray obey repent formed Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: It is labor, cold, hunger, silence, services in the night, and the slow death of self-importance.  Paisios entered that world and stayed close to its hardest le...

beats.search_query: mountain hides labor cold hunger silence services Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: Later, when people came to him, they were not meeting a spiritual celebrity.  They were meeting a man whose words had been tested by solitude.

scenes.title: Let the Soul Thirst
scenes.visual_prompt: Candlelit Athonite cell, cup of water beside prayer rope, dry earth outside window receiving first rain, symbolic but restrained.

beats.narration_excerpt: One remembered conversation turns around a simple phrase: let the soul always thirst for God.  The point was not emotional excitement.  It was hunger.
beats.asset_note: Scene anchor: use the saint portrait/icon as a reverent recurring visual.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos icon portrait Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: A soul can be stuffed with distractions and still be starving.  Paisios warned people against letting the heart become satisfied with substitutes.  Comfort can ...
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: soul thirst stuffed distractions starving paisios warned Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: Prayer, repentance, and love for God were not decorations added to life.  They were water.  The elder's counsel was gentle, but it was also severe.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: soul thirst prayer repentance love decorations added Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: If the soul stops thirsting, it begins to accept almost anything as life.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: soul thirst stops thirsting begins accept almost Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

scenes.title: Poverty Without Bitterness
scenes.visual_prompt: Sparse monastic cell: wooden stool, icons, oil lamp, prayer rope, patched clothing, morning light through small window.

beats.narration_excerpt: Paisios' poverty was not theatrical.  It was freedom.  He lived simply because he wanted the heart to be available to God and neighbor.
beats.asset_note: Opening/scene anchor: use Mount Athos path, cell, monastery, sea, or reverent Athonite atmosphere.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: In a world trained to measure value by comfort and visibility, that kind of poverty becomes a rebuke.  His hut was small.  His possessions were few.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: poverty without bitterness world trained measure value Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: But people did not leave feeling that they had met an empty man.  They left feeling that they had met someone who had made room inside himself for others.  The ...
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic

scenes.title: The Hidden Labor of Love

beats.narration_excerpt: The work of an elder can look simple from the outside.  A visitor arrives.  A few words are exchanged.

beats.narration_excerpt: A blessing is given.  But behind that moment is prayer, fasting, illness, exhaustion, and the daily struggle not to love people in theory, but in front of you. ...

beats.narration_excerpt: He did not solve every problem.  He did not remove every suffering.  But he taught people how to suffer without becoming closed, how to repent without despair, ...

scenes.title: A Saint for the Restless
scenes.visual_prompt: Mount Athos sunrise, empty bench outside elder's hut, pilgrims walking away in silence, sea and sky opening softly.

beats.narration_excerpt: Saint Paisios reposed in 1994, but his memory spread quickly because the wounds he addressed were not old wounds only.  Restlessness, distraction, fear, spiritu...

beats.search_query: Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: This is why people continue to read his counsels and seek his prayers.  He speaks to an age that has forgotten how to be quiet.

beats.search_query: saint restless continue read counsels seek prayers Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: Not because quiet is empty, but because quiet is where the soul finally hears what it has been thirsting for.

scenes.title: The Water Beneath the Noise
scenes.visual_prompt: Final cinematic image of water spring near an Athonite path, candle reflection, distant monastery bells, title reveal: The Saints.

beats.narration_excerpt: The story of Saint Paisios is not a command to run away from the world.  It is an invitation to stop letting the world define what your soul needs.
beats.asset_note: Opening/scene anchor: use Mount Athos path, cell, monastery, sea, or reverent Athonite atmosphere.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: The elder on the mountain reminds us that holiness is not noise, branding, or control.  It is thirst rightly directed.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: water beneath noise elder mountain reminds holiness Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: It is love purified by prayer.  It is the courage to become simple before God.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: water beneath noise love purified prayer courage Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

scenes.title: Why They Still Climb
scenes.visual_prompt: Pilgrims climbing a quiet Athonite path at sunrise, sea mist, monastery bells, silence after modern noise, cinematic but restrained.

beats.narration_excerpt: People still return to Saint Paisios because his life exposes a modern wound.  We are surrounded by answers and still starving for wisdom.
beats.asset_note: Scene anchor: use the saint portrait/icon as a reverent recurring visual.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos icon portrait Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: We are connected to everyone and still lonely.  We can explain ourselves endlessly and still not know how to repent, forgive, pray, or suffer well.
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: climb connected everyone lonely explain ourselves endlessly Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons

beats.narration_excerpt: Paisios does not offer nostalgia for an easier past.  He offers a harder and more merciful question: what if the soul is restless because it is thirsty for God?
beats.primary_source_label: Saint Paisios of Mount Athos mosaic
beats.search_query: climb paisios does offer nostalgia easier past Mount Athos monastery path cell Wikimedia Commons
